                                                                                                                                {"props":{"commentPermalink":"https://github.com/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#pullrequestreview-4394206736","isCopilotAuthoredPr":false,"isOverviewComment":true,"commentCount":16,"threadComments":[{"filename":"core/src/commonMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/core/domain/auth/FirebaseAuthClient.kt","message":"`kotlinx.coroutines.IO` is not a valid import and will fail compilation; `Dispatchers.IO` is already used and does not require this import.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932730","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003e\u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003ekotlinx.coroutines.IO\u003c/code\u003e is not a valid import and will fail compilation; \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eDispatchers.IO\u003c/code\u003e is already used and does not require this import.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"core/src/commonMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/core/domain/auth/FirebaseAuthClient.kt","message":"`refreshToken` is inserted into an `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` body without URL-encoding. Refresh tokens can contain characters that must be encoded, otherwise the request can be malformed and token refresh will intermittently fail.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932739","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003e\u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003erefreshToken\u003c/code\u003e is inserted into an \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded\u003c/code\u003e body without URL-encoding. Refresh tokens can contain characters that must be encoded, otherwise the request can be malformed and token refresh will intermittently fail.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"core/src/commonMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/core/domain/auth/FirebaseAuthClient.kt","message":"Request bodies for callable functions are built via string interpolation without JSON escaping. `deviceName`, `deviceId`, `nonce`, etc. can contain quotes/backslashes and produce invalid JSON (or allow request-shaping if any value is attacker-controlled). Use `kotlinx.serialization` / `buildJsonObject` to construct the payload instead of manual strings.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932742","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003eRequest bodies for callable functions are built via string interpolation without JSON escaping. \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003edeviceName\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003edeviceId\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003enonce\u003c/code\u003e, etc. can contain quotes/backslashes and produce invalid JSON (or allow request-shaping if any value is attacker-controlled). Use \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003ekotlinx.serialization\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003ebuildJsonObject\u003c/code\u003e to construct the payload instead of manual strings.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"core/src/desktopMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/core/auth/LocalAuthServer.kt","message":"The loopback auth server is currently created with `InetSocketAddress(0)`, which binds to the wildcard address (all interfaces) on many platforms. That exposes the `/callback` endpoint to the local network, allowing remote requests to hit the callback handler (token injection/denial-of-service). Bind explicitly to the loopback address.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932747","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003eThe loopback auth server is currently created with \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eInetSocketAddress(0)\u003c/code\u003e, which binds to the wildcard address (all interfaces) on many platforms. That exposes the \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003e/callback\u003c/code\u003e endpoint to the local network, allowing remote requests to hit the callback handler (token injection/denial-of-service). Bind explicitly to the loopback address.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"core/src/desktopMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/core/auth/LocalAuthServer.kt","message":"The auth flow expects `idToken`/`refreshToken` to arrive as URL query parameters. That leaks credentials into browser history, OS URL logs, and potentially referrer logs if any intermediate navigation happens. Prefer a one-time auth code exchanged by the app, or at least POST the tokens in the request body and clear them from the URL via an intermediate page.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932755","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003eThe auth flow expects \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eidToken\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003erefreshToken\u003c/code\u003e to arrive as URL query parameters. That leaks credentials into browser history, OS URL logs, and potentially referrer logs if any intermediate navigation happens. Prefer a one-time auth code exchanged by the app, or at least POST the tokens in the request body and clear them from the URL via an intermediate page.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"app-desktop/src/desktopMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/desktop/SettingsManager.kt","message":"`getDeviceId()` derives the device identifier from `os.name`, `user.home`, and `user.name`. This (1) includes PII-like data (home path/user name) in a value that is later sent to the backend, and (2) is not stable across OS user/hostname/home directory changes. Persist a generated UUID once and reuse it.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932766","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003e\u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003egetDeviceId()\u003c/code\u003e derives the device identifier from \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eos.name\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003euser.home\u003c/code\u003e, and \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003euser.name\u003c/code\u003e. This (1) includes PII-like data (home path/user name) in a value that is later sent to the backend, and (2) is not stable across OS user/hostname/home directory changes. Persist a generated UUID once and reuse it.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"app-desktop/src/desktopMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/desktop/SettingsManager.kt","message":"Firebase ID tokens and (especially) refresh tokens are being stored in `java.util.prefs.Preferences`, which is typically plaintext and user-readable. A stolen refresh token effectively grants ongoing account access; these should be stored in OS-provided secure storage (Keychain / Credential Manager / libsecret) rather than Preferences.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932773","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003eFirebase ID tokens and (especially) refresh tokens are being stored in \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003ejava.util.prefs.Preferences\u003c/code\u003e, which is typically plaintext and user-readable. A stolen refresh token effectively grants ongoing account access; these should be stored in OS-provided secure storage (Keychain / Credential Manager / libsecret) rather than Preferences.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"app-desktop/src/desktopMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/desktop/SettingsDialog.kt","message":"Unused imports (`Date`, `Base64`) were added and are not referenced anywhere in this file; they should be removed to keep the import list clean.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932782","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003eUnused imports (\u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eDate\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eBase64\u003c/code\u003e) were added and are not referenced anywhere in this file; they should be removed to keep the import list clean.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"app-desktop/src/desktopMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/desktop/SettingsDialog.kt","message":"This connection flow writes a placeholder `weights.bin` file with mock contents into the user's home directory. Shipping code that writes fake model data can overwrite real model files, confuses troubleshooting, and creates unnecessary disk writes. Prefer either (a) no-op here until a real download/provisioning implementation exists, or (b) call a real model downloader and verify integrity.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932789","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003eThis connection flow writes a placeholder \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eweights.bin\u003c/code\u003e file with mock contents into the user's home directory. Shipping code that writes fake model data can overwrite real model files, confuses troubleshooting, and creates unnecessary disk writes. Prefer either (a) no-op here until a real download/provisioning implementation exists, or (b) call a real model downloader and verify integrity.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"app-desktop/src/desktopMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/desktop/Main.kt","message":"`ParserMode` is imported but not referenced directly in this file (the inferred type from `getEffectiveParserMode()` doesn't require the import). Removing unused imports avoids noise and keeps warnings down.","permalink":"/LookAtWhatAiCanDo/Codeoba/pull/1#discussion_r3327932802","hasSuggestion":true,"suggestionHtml":"\u003cp\u003e\u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eParserMode\u003c/code\u003e is imported but not referenced directly in this file (the inferred type from \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003egetEffectiveParserMode()\u003c/code\u003e doesn't require the import). Removing unused imports avoids noise and keeps warnings down.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"filename":"app-desktop/src/desktopMain/kotlin/llc/lookatwhataicando/codeoba/desktop/DetailPane.kt","message":"These imports (`ParserMode`, `PerformanceChartPoint`, `LogParserFactory`) are not used in this file after the changes. Pull request overview

This PR introduces the foundations of Codeoba’s multi-device “Ecosystem” experience: a browser-based authentication handshake via a local loopback callback server, device identity keypairs + challenge signing for device registration, client-side secret scrubbing prior to sync, and new Settings UI/keys to control sync mode, exclusions, remote-control policy, and log parser mode (standard vs AI summarizing).
Changes:
Add local loopback auth server + Firebase callable client to support browser sign-in and device registration challenge/response.
Add device keypair management + secrets redaction utility (with tests) and extend session model to include AI summaries.
Extend Desktop Settings UI/Preferences with Ecosystem controls (sync mode, exclusions, remote policy, parser mode) and update docs describing the subscription/ecosystem architecture.

`refreshToken` is inserted into an `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` body without URL-encoding. Refresh tokens can contain characters that must be encoded, otherwise the request can be malformed and token refresh will intermittently fail.

`refreshToken` is inserted into an `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` body without URL-encoding. Refresh tokens can contain characters that must be encoded, otherwise the request can be malformed and token refresh will intermittently fail.

Request bodies for callable functions are built via string interpolation without JSON escaping. `deviceName`, `deviceId`, `nonce`, etc. can contain quotes/backslashes and produce invalid JSON (or allow request-shaping if any value is attacker-controlled). Use `kotlinx.serialization` / `buildJsonObject` to construct the payload instead of manual strings.

Request bodies for callable functions are built via string interpolation without JSON escaping. `deviceName`, `deviceId`, `nonce`, etc. can contain quotes/backslashes and produce invalid JSON (or allow request-shaping if any value is attacker-controlled). Use `kotlinx.serialization` / `buildJsonObject` to construct the payload instead of manual strings.

The loopback auth server is currently created with `InetSocketAddress(0)`, which binds to the wildcard address (all interfaces) on many platforms. That exposes the `/callback` endpoint to the local network, allowing remote requests to hit the callback handler (token injection/denial-of-service). Bind explicitly to the loopback address.

The loopback auth server is currently created with `InetSocketAddress(0)`, which binds to the wildcard address (all interfaces) on many platforms. That exposes the `/callback` endpoint to the local network, allowing remote requests to hit the callback handler (token injection/denial-of-service). Bind explicitly to the loopback address.

The auth flow expects `idToken`/`refreshToken` to arrive as URL query parameters. That leaks credentials into browser history, OS URL logs, and potentially referrer logs if any intermediate navigation happens. Prefer a one-time auth code exchanged by the app, or at least POST the tokens in the request body and clear them from the URL via an intermediate page.

The auth flow expects `idToken`/`refreshToken` to arrive as URL query parameters. That leaks credentials into browser history, OS URL logs, and potentially referrer logs if any intermediate navigation happens. Prefer a one-time auth code exchanged by the app, or at least POST the tokens in the request body and clear them from the URL via an intermediate page.

`getDeviceId()` derives the device identifier from `os.name`, `user.home`, and `user.name`. This (1) includes PII-like data (home path/user name) in a value that is later sent to the backend, and (2) is not stable across OS user/hostname/home directory changes. Persist a generated UUID once and reuse it.

`getDeviceId()` derives the device identifier from `os.name`, `user.home`, and `user.name`. This (1) includes PII-like data (home path/user name) in a value that is later sent to the backend, and (2) is not stable across OS user/hostname/home directory changes. Persist a generated UUID once and reuse it.

Firebase ID tokens and (especially) refresh tokens are being stored in `java.util.prefs.Preferences`, which is typically plaintext and user-readable. A stolen refresh token effectively grants ongoing account access; these should be stored in OS-provided secure storage (Keychain / Credential Manager / libsecret) rather than Preferences.

Firebase ID tokens and (especially) refresh tokens are being stored in `java.util.prefs.Preferences`, which is typically plaintext and user-readable. A stolen refresh token effectively grants ongoing account access; these should be stored in OS-provided secure storage (Keychain / Credential Manager / libsecret) rather than Preferences.

This connection flow writes a placeholder `weights.bin` file with mock contents into the user's home directory. Shipping code that writes fake model data can overwrite real model files, confuses troubleshooting, and creates unnecessary disk writes. Prefer either (a) no-op here until a real download/provisioning implementation exists, or (b) call a real model downloader and verify integrity.

This connection flow writes a placeholder `weights.bin` file with mock contents into the user's home directory. Shipping code that writes fake model data can overwrite real model files, confuses troubleshooting, and creates unnecessary disk writes. Prefer either (a) no-op here until a real download/provisioning implementation exists, or (b) call a real model downloader and verify integrity.

When `ParserMode.SUMMARIZING` is enabled, the cache write happens inside the delegate (before summarization), so cache hits still trigger `LocalModelRunner.runLocalInference(...)` on every `parseSession` call. That negates the cache benefit for summarization and can make rescans expensive. Consider caching the final parsed `Session` (including `summary`) or bypassing summarization when a cached summarized session is available.

When `ParserMode.SUMMARIZING` is enabled, the cache write happens inside the delegate (before summarization), so cache hits still trigger `LocalModelRunner.runLocalInference(...)` on every `parseSession` call. That negates the cache benefit for summarization and can make rescans expensive. Consider caching the final parsed `Session` (including `summary`) or bypassing summarization when a cached summarized session is available.

Query parsing uses `it.split("=")` without a limit. If any parameter value contains `=` (common in URL-encoded/base64-ish strings), `parts.size` will be >2 and the value will be dropped, breaking the auth callback. Use `split("=", limit = 2)` so values can safely contain `=`.

Query parsing uses `it.split("=")` without a limit. If any parameter value contains `=` (common in URL-encoded/base64-ish strings), `parts.size` will be >2 and the value will be dropped, breaking the auth callback. Use `split("=", limit = 2)` so values can safely contain `=`.

This guide states that “AI-powered conversation summaries” are free by default, but the implementation in `SettingsDialog` presents AI summarization as a Premium-only parser mode (disabled when not subscribed). Please align the documentation with the actual entitlement behavior to avoid misleading users.

This section says “free AI summaries are enabled by default for all users,” but the UI/logic in this PR treats AI-powered log summarization as a Premium feature (see the Premium badge and disabled selection when `ecosystem_active` is false). Please update this bullet to match the implemented subscription gating.
